title: arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. viscida
intro: Arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. viscida, also known as sticky-thyme or sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is commonly found in dry, sandy areas and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. viscida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
